Ad Text:
Are you looking for an awesome place to work, where you can proudly be your authentic self, and be part of #oneteam?

We are looking for a passionate team player who aligns with our values and culture, takes pride in their unique contributions, and can challenge the status quo with disruptive thinking. If this sounds like you, come and join us!

Reporting to the Head of FP&A, this role is responsible for but not limited to the delivery of the monthly performance reporting, annual budgeting cycle and monthly rolling forecasts.


Working alongside other FP&A Analysts and the Head of FP&A, you will provide insightful reporting of business performance and play a key role in aligning business strategy and financial objectives.

Responsibilities

Monthly reporting and analysis - management reporting including Monthly Business Review and Board Pack preparation, insightful variance reporting and analysis, building and driving continuous improvement of financial reporting
Management of risks and opportunities and appraisal of impact on financial performance and budget
Business Partnering - supporting immediate business stakeholders, finance stakeholders and direct manager
Budgeting and Forecasting - build and maintain robust budget/forecast models, involving in annual budget cycles and monthly rolling forecasts
Undertaking financial and commercial assessments for the business arising from business decisions and initiatives.

Requirements

5-7+ years’ experience, preferably with experience in the FMCG/ telco/tech industry
CA/CPA qualification
Very detail-oriented, proficient in financial modelling and analysis, and possesses excellent communication skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams
Strong accounting and commercial skills with thorough understanding of the annual planning and forecasting cycle
Strong relationship building skills with experience in the effective management of diverse internal and external stakeholder groups
Advanced Excel skills.

What We Offer 

Working at Vocus will give you rewarding experiences and the opportunity to do extraordinary work. You will enjoy: 

Diverse and dynamic teams with a supportive and inclusive culture. 
Supportive career development plans with comprehensive ongoing training, support, and development opportunities. 
Generous discounts to over 400 retailers. 
Competitive leave options, including anniversary leave, purchased leave, parental leave, volunteering leave, study leave, and 5 extra days of Vocus leave each year, plus more.
Study assistance programs to excel your personal growth, learning and development. 
Health and wellness offerings, including access to our wellbeing initiatives that can help you from a financial, psychological, and physical perspective. 

Working at Vocus is never just a job – it’s personal. We’re crazy about our customers and believe our people are the difference. Our wonderfully diverse team and a vibrant culture define us. We care about the impact we have on our people, our customers, and our communities and are committed to operating as a responsible, ethical business. 

We pride ourselves on setting audacious and ambitious goals and believe that we can play our part in changing the Australian telecommunications landscape and make a fundamental difference to people’s lives. 

With us, you’ll have the opportunity to lead and inspire teams, work on projects that are shaping the future of telecommunications and become part of a culture that thrives on creativity, encourages new ideas, and provides a collaborative and inclusive environment.  

About Us 

As Australia’s specialist fibre and network solutions provider, we own and operate 25,000km of secure, high-capacity fibre connecting people, businesses, governments, and communities across Australia to the world. Through our well-known retail brands, we deliver simple and affordable broadband, mobile, voice and energy services with the purpose of Building Critical Connections. Enabling Better Possibilities.  

Ready to take the next step? 

If you like the sound of this role and think you’d do a great job, but are worried you don’t tick every box, we encourage you to back yourself – we know that diverse groups are less-likely to apply for roles they’re not 100% qualified for, but just as likely to succeed at them!  

We are a proud equal opportunity employer committed to providing a safe, diverse, and inclusive working environment where all our team members feel like they belong. We know diversity makes us stronger and we encourage applications irrespective of background, age, origin, gender, sexual orientation, identity, or ability.   

If you feel comfortable, please let us know if you have any accessibility requirements upon application, so we can make any adjustments required to support you throughout our recruitment process.

A Melbourne-based superannuation organisation is seeking an Assurance Analyst - Technology to join their Operations Assurance team on a permanent basis. This is a newly created role focused on strengthening Line 1 (L1) risk and compliance across technology.

You’ll act as the first line of defence, working closely with business and technology teams to ensure controls are operating effectively, risks are understood, and compliance obligations are met.

It’s hands-on, varied, and gives you exposure across cyber, data, resilience, and incident governance - ideal for someone who wants to build real depth in technology risk.

Key Responsibilities:
Perform control testing, assurance activities, and compliance reviews across technology and operations
Support quarterly control self-assessments and ensure obligations are met on time
Act as a first point of contact (SME) for risk and compliance queries from the business
Identify gaps, weaknesses, and risks across systems, processes, and controls
Support business units in addressing internal audit findings and remediation activities
Assist in building and refining assurance methodologies, frameworks, and procedures
Oversee incident governance processes – triage, risk rating, ownership and closure reviews
Provide a bridge between L1 and L2 risk, escalating where required
About You:
Experience in technology risk, IT audit, or controls testing
Strong understanding of GRC / risk & compliance frameworks
Exposure to highly-regulated environments (Superannuation or Financial Services preferred)
Comfortable working in a hands-on, operational role
Strong stakeholder skills - able to support and guide non-risk teams
Certifications such as CISA / CISSP are advantageous
Why This Role
Newly created position with real scope to shape how L1 operates
Broad exposure across technology risk domains
Development pathway into senior risk or L2 roles
Reach out for a confidential conversation with Jodie Marron, Senior Principal Consultant, on 0413 900 039 or jodie@frazertremble.com.au, or apply directly.

Company Description

Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description

Join Nine as our Workday Cross-Functional Lead, a senior strategic role driving the digital transformation of our People & Culture ecosystem. 

After a successful implementation process, Nine is on the verge of going live with Workday HRIS.  As Workday Cross-Functional Lead, you will lead Nine’s internal Functional Support team.  You will drive the integration and continuous improvement of Workday, translating complex business strategies into scalable, high-impact technology solutions. 

This is a unique opportunity to lead a talented functional team and shape the future of work at Nine during a landmark enterprise-wide evolution.

Day to day you will:

Lead a small team of internal Workday Functional Analysts, overseeing their technical development and prioritising daily support and project delivery.
Drive system governance and platform health, ensuring data integrity, security compliance, and the successful execution of semi-annual Workday releases.
Manage the external AMS provider relationship, acting as the primary liaison for complex Tier 3 escalations and system enhancement specifications.
Maintain the platform roadmap in collaboration with Technology and business leaders to drive relentless process optimisation and automation.
Provide deep functional expertise across Core HCM, Recruitment, Compensation, and Talent modules to resolve escalated configuration challenges.
Provide advice to the Workday Phase 2 implementation team, which will launch Advanced Compensation and Performance modules in July 2026
Qualifications

What you'll bring:

Extensive Workday expertise in a Functional Lead, Architect, or Senior Analyst capacity
Advanced configuration skills across multiple modules, such as Core HCM, Recruitment, Compensation, Talent, and Absence Management.
Proven people leadership capabilities, with a track record of mentoring teams and managing complex workloads effectively.
Strong vendor management experience, specifically in governing external Application Management Services (AMS) and technical partners.
Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to translate intricate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and executives.
Analytical rigour and a high attention to detail for managing end-to-end testing, deployment, and system documentation.
Additional Information

How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers.
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

The Company
High growth, ASX Listed Infrastructure business with a strategy for continued growth through organic means and acquisitions. 

This organisation is a clear market leader and key to this success is investing in their people, technology and excellence in delivery for their customers. 

This role is a great entry point into a business that is growing, delivering shareholder value and provides multiple career development pathways.

About the Role
As a Senior Tax Analyst you will play a critical role in ensuring financial operations are streamlined and compliant with all tax regulations.

You will also provide support to the wider Finance function and Business in relation to all Taxation matters as well as lead process improvement projects within the Tax function.

Key Responsibilities

Responsible for all tax compliance obligations for both Australia and New Zealand 
Preparation of income tax returns
Provide advice to business stakeholders on GST and FBT matters
GST, FBT, Withholding tax and FTC reporting
Support with statutory tax reporting, budgeting and forecasting obligations
Conduct reviews of business and system processes and make process improvement recommendations.
Assist with the update and implementation of changes to the Tax Corporate Governance Framework
Skills and Experience Required 

CA / CPA Qualified
4+ years tax experience – Big 4, business services or corporate
Demonstrated Australian tax technical ability, exposure to complex corporates beneficial
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Ability to identify process improvement opportunities and implement efficiencies
Don't hesitate to apply!

If you are confident you have the skills and personality outlined above I am keen to hear from you. To apply please forward your resume to Appreciating Talent TODAY using the links below. We look forward to hearing from you.

Head of Equites
  
A rare and influential leadership opportunity has arisen for a highly experienced equities research executive to lead and shape a high‑performing research function across both Australian and International Equities. This role is ideal for a strategic thinker who thrives at the intersection of qualitative insight, quantitative rigour and portfolio construction leadership.
  
The Role
  

Lead the investment research function that blends traditional fundamental analysis with robust quantitative tools and screening methodologies.
Oversee portfolio construction across both Australian and International Equities, ensuring alignment with house philosophy, risk parameters, and investment objectives.
Refine the firm’s portfolio construction philosophy, including frameworks for risk assessment, stress testing, and performance monitoring.
Drive continual improvement in research standards, adopting global best practices and championing the effective use of technology, data, and analytics.
Manage, mentor, and develop the Australian Equity Research team, fostering a collaborative and high‑accountability culture.
Partner with senior investment leaders to influence strategy, optimise processes, and contribute to superior investment outcomes.
  
The Person

Extensive experience applying quantitative frameworks, screening tools, and analytical methods within an equities research or portfolio management environment.
Strong technical understanding of risk modelling, scenario analysis, and stress testing, and how these support portfolio construction and ongoing oversight.
Proven leadership capability with experience managing senior analysts or research teams.
A track record of improving research processes, raising analytical standards, and embedding technology‑driven enhancements.
At least 15 years' experience in equities research, portfolio construction, or related investment leadership roles.
Excellent communication, stakeholder influence, and the ability to operate at executive and Board‑facing levels.

Senior Advanced Software Engineer

Location – Perth (WA)




Start your career by making an impact and real connections with some of the most meaningful challenges around. When you join Honeywell, you become a member of our performance culture comprised of diverse leaders, thinkers, innovators, dreamers, and doers who are changing the future. 




Make the Best You.

Working at Honeywell is not just creating incredible things. You will collaborate with top minds, grow through continuous learning, and thrive in an environment that rewards and celebrates achievements.




Join Us and Make an Impact.

 

Snr Advanced Software Engineer (Historian) is responsible for applying expert knowledge of technologies relevant to the Honeywell and competitor Historian products space to provide first class support, improve product quality, distribute product knowledge, and deliver product innovation through software development tasks – to ultimately make our solutions more successful.




Key Responsibilities:




Develop expert understanding of selected PHD/Enterprise Historian subsystems. Develop product enhancements through a range of activities across the full software development lifecycle

Develop, maintain, and extend automated test infrastructure

Contribute to test execution for PHD releases and updates

First or second line PHD developer level support to GTAC and to PHD development team

Contribute to innovation ideas, technology exploration and implementation in Honeywell historian offerings

Contribute to product user and technical documentation, knowledge sharing articles and next generation user assistance solutions

Key Experience & Capabilities:




Tertiary qualification in engineering, computer science or equivalent discipline

Established expertise with Honeywell PHD, Experion DCS, or EBI (3 years minimum of hands-on experience) ideally with history subsystem knowledge

3+ years software experience

Familiar with analysis, design, coding, testing and support of software




Who We Are

The Future Is What We Make It at Honeywell. From sustainable aviation fuel and life-saving healthcare sensors to collaborating on every NASA space mission since the 1950’s, over 100 years of innovation has always been driven by an investment in our people. Learn more about Honeywell: youtube.com/watch?v=CG-rmG0eKLk 




Discover More 

Our focus at Honeywell is innovation that drives business, improves the bottom line and creates solutions for our customers and communities around the world. There’s a lot for you to discover. Our solutions, our case studies, our #Futureshapers, and so much more.

Company: UGL - Services - Transport Manufacturing and Maintenance
Ad Text:
Shape the future of passenger rail transport
Opportunities for career growth & development 
Tier 1 Engineering Services Company


We’re moving Australia forward and we want you to move with us. Want to make a real impact, join a dynamic team, be part of something that depend on – Transport. We believe success and value are driven by real teams where everyone has a role to play.

Our people work on collaborative and exciting projects, shape the way we evolve, adapt and transform the industries that we work in. We rally together, drawing on the diverse skills across our teams and create environments where great ideas are heard. 

About the Project: 

Join UGL Limited’s Manufacturing and Maintenance division as we embark on the landmark New Intercity Fleet (NIF) project. This initiative will revolutionise train services for customers travelling from Sydney to the Central Coast, Newcastle, the Blue Mountains, and the South Coast. 

About Your Role:

The role of Fleet Performance Analyst is to drive operational efficiency and effectiveness within RailConnect, ensuring precise attribution of in-service incidents and failures in collaboration with Transport for NSW and consortium partners. This role is pivotal in managing technical investigations, optimising workflows, and enhancing maintenance strategies to deliver a seamless operational environment.

Key Responsibilities:
 

Lead Investigations: Conduct technical and attribution investigations regarding in-service incidents and failures, providing insightful analysis and solutions.
Risk and Cost Analysis: Engage in discussions to identify potential risks and costs associated with delays and availability challenges proactively.
Report Development: Collaborate with the Performance Engineering Lead to develop comprehensive attribution reports, which aid in managing and disputing claims effectively.
Contractual Expertise: Display a deep understanding of the contractual obligations and requirements outlined in the consortium deed to ensure precise compliance and communication.
Technical Proficiency: Maintain a thorough understanding of necessary subsystems for effective interpretation of NIF subsystem logs.
Data Management: Keep detailed and accurate records of attribution investigations to apply trending and RAM (Reliability, Availability, Maintainability) practices to incident data.
Daily Meetings Participation: Attend and contribute to daily attribution meetings, ensuring accurate allocation of operational incidents and assessment of penalties.


About You: 

As the Fleet Performance Analyst, you will have mechanical, electrical engineering or equivalent technical and practical experience, particularly within the rollingstock maintenance field. You must also have a deep understanding of train operations and electric passenger rolling stock systems.
 

Analytical / Critical Thinking: Ability to analyse complex information critically and identify patterns to make well-informed decisions.
Attention to Detail: Requires meticulous monitoring of incidents to ensure precise attribution of responsibility.
Communication Skills: Necessitates effective communication with various stakeholders, engaging in discussions and resolving discrepancies in incident attribution.
Team Collaboration: Ability to work collaboratively with other consortium partners to investigate incidents and streamline attribution processes
Technical Proficiency: Experienced in the rail, automotive, military, defense, avionics, or other safety-critical environments, showcasing adaptability and expertise in high-stakes industries.
Qualifications:  Bachelor's degree in Mechanical, Electrical Engineering or equivalent technical and practical experience.


Our Benefits:
 

Work-life Balance: At UGL, we understand the importance of maintaining a balance between professional and personal life. We provide flexible working hours and are understanding about family commitments.
Equal Opportunities: We are an equal opportunity employer and ensure that everyone has the same opportunities for career advancement and skill development.
Supportive Environment: Our workplace culture nurtures mutual respect and understanding. We believe in treating every individual fairly and firmly discourage any form of discrimination or harassment.
Health & Well-being: We understand the wellness needs of our employees by supporting a range of health and wellness programs – AIA Vitality, Salary Continuance, Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Professional Development: Immerse yourself in a role offering growth and learning opportunities within a supportive team environment.
Impactful Work: Contribute to essential services that directly enhance transportation reliability and customer satisfaction.


About Us

UGL is a well-known and respected for providing end-to-end engineering, construction and maintenance services within; rail and transport, communications and technology systems, oil and gas, power, renewables and resources, water and defence markets. UGL partners with some of the world’s largest blue-chip companies and government agencies, private enterprises and public institutions. We are a member of the CIMIC Group of companies.

At UGL, we value diversity – in the backgrounds, ideas, work styles and perspectives of our team. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and strongly encourage people with disability, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander people, mature aged workers and people from culturally diverse backgrounds to apply.

Please note that relevant screening checks will be conducted as part of the recruitment process. These may include a national criminal history check and for you to undergo pre-employment medical assessment including drug and alcohol screen.
